PUMPFUNUSDT.P.proposed_type: Active Short (entered, against a Watch Only/Skip rating)
PUMPFUNUSDT.P.bias: From the morning mover scan (Bybit +12.9% gainer, $63.1M turnover). Chart-verified via Bybit 4H/15m kline. PUMPFUN has been range-bound 0.00135-0.0017 for two weeks. Price bounced off the range low and retested the range-top resistance cluster (0.00165-0.0017, tested repeatedly Jul 5-7). CORRECTION (this pass): the first TP pick skipped over a major intervening zone - a 0.00147-0.00153 band that was PUMPFUN's actual multi-day base (Jul 8-14, dozens of touches). A corrected TP Zone Validity Rule check at the real near edge (0.0015244) drops TP1's R:R from 2.4R to 1.26R, below the Intraday 2R floor - setup was downgraded to WAIT/SKIP. User entered anyway before/independent of seeing that correction: limit resting at 0.00165, confirmed on the 15m chart - real rejection candle at 12:30 UTC+1 (touched 0.0016641, closed 0.0016378, below the line), reinforced at 12:45 (closed 0.0016256), then a retest wick back to 0.0016591 at 13:45 UTC+1 filled the resting limit, closing at 0.0016469. Execution technique was correct (limit at the zone edge, filled on the retest of an already-confirmed rejection, not a market chase) - the entry mechanics were sound, the underlying R:R was not.
PUMPFUNUSDT.P.btc_eth_filter: Not re-verified this specific pass; last known BTC +3.37%/ETH +5.24% (aligned bullish) from earlier today.

entry.basis: Range-top resistance cluster, tested repeatedly Jul 5-7 (highs to 0.0017001), confirmed-rejected at 12:30 UTC+1 (close 0.0016378, below the line), limit filled on the 13:45 retest at 0.0016469. Even on a clean confirmed rejection, the trade fails R:R at the correct TP1 - taken anyway, now being managed as a live, sub-floor position.

PUMPFUNUSDT.P.invalidation: A close above 0.001727 (clearing the 0.0017001 cluster high with a volatility-derived buffer, not a borrowed percentage) invalidates the short.
PUMPFUNUSDT.P.nearest_liquidity: Buy-side above 0.0017001 (the range high, untested air above); sell-side, in order: the 0.0014948-0.0015244 base zone (Jul 8-14, dozens of touches - the real first zone, previously missed), then the smaller 0.00138-0.00141 cluster (Jul 11-12), then the single Jul 14 wick low at 0.0013521 (not a confirmed zone, lowest confidence).
PUMPFUNUSDT.P.reclaim_status: Rejected and filled: real confirmation at 12:30 UTC+1, retested and filled at 13:45 UTC+1. No reclaim attempt since.
PUMPFUNUSDT.P.relative_strength: Outperformer today (+12.9%) but this is a bounce within an established range, not fresh momentum - the coin has round-tripped this same 0.00135-0.0017 band multiple times over two weeks without breaking out.
PUMPFUNUSDT.P.final_action: MANAGE, EYES OPEN - this trade is live but was taken below the R:R floor (1.63R at the real TP1 vs. the 2R Intraday floor). Do not treat TP1 (0.0015244) as a full-conviction target the way a floor-clearing trade would be; it's the correct near-edge level, it just doesn't pay enough to justify the risk on its own. Manage size/expectations accordingly rather than trailing SL as if this were a clean setup.
PUMPFUNUSDT.P.notes: 2026-07-15 first analysis. Zone boundaries verified via Bybit 4H kline data. TP1 originally placed at 0.00141 (a real but SECOND zone), skipping the actual first zone (0.0014948-0.0015244, the coin's own multi-day base) entirely - the same span-past-the-first-zone error as JTO's original TP1, caught by the user pointing at the specific price band. Corrected: tp[0]=0.0015244 (real target), tp[1]=0.00141 (conditional stretch), tp[2]=0.00135 (lowest confidence). Pattern named explicitly: every zone-precision pass this session (JTO x2, US, BSB, PUMPFUN) has moved R:R DOWN from the first-pass estimate, never up. 2026-07-15 14:04 UTC follow-up: user confirmed a real limit entry, filled 13:45 UTC+1 at 0.00165 - reconciled against 15m Bybit data (real confirmation candle at 12:30, predating this ledger's analyzed_at anchor of 12:48:53, which is why the Pine card only tagged the 13:45 retest-fill as 'ENTRY TAGGED' rather than the earlier true confirmation). Entry mechanics (limit at zone edge, filled on retest, not chased at market) were textbook-correct per this session's own established process; the R:R shortfall is a separate, pre-existing issue. Position updated to ENTERED SHORT / ACTIVE MANAGE despite the Skip rating - taken trades get tracked honestly regardless of whether they should have been taken, same principle as JTO's early-entry record. 2026-07-15 14:44 UTC SL recalibration: the SL buffer (previously 0.00175, ~2.94% above the wick) was flagged by the user as suspiciously identical to JTO's (~2.63%) and US's (~3.0%) buffers despite these three coins having very different realized volatility - checked directly (avg 4H true range: JTO 3.53%, US 8.45%, PUMPFUN 3.61% of price) and confirmed the buffer had NOT actually been derived per-coin, it was an unstated borrowed heuristic dressed up with coin-specific wick numbers. Recomputed properly: buffer = 50% of PUMPFUN's own avg 4H true range (5.36e-5 absolute, ~1.58% of price) above the 0.0017001 wick high, giving SL 0.001727. R:R improves from 1.26R to 1.63R at TP1 - still fails the 2R floor, verdict unchanged, but the number is now honestly derived rather than copied from a prior coin.

USUSDT.P.proposed_type: Proposed Wait / Parabolic - No Base Yet
USUSDT.P.bias: REGIME CHANGE (2026-07-15, live TradingView, wall-clock confirmed): the short thesis is fully dead. Since the last check (~0.0252, no approach to the zone), price has exploded through the ENTIRE 0.027-0.0309 structure - the original entry zone, the reclaim line, and the SL - in one violent move, now trading ~0.0332 (+22% today per the live ticker, matches Bybit's independent +23% gainer-list read). The card's own Live row confirms: SL TAGGED, Jul 15 07:30. This is not a rejection-then-reclaim sequence, it is a single explosive parabolic candle blowing straight through the old resistance cluster with no pause. Per the Parabolic Spike Rule, this rules out any blind long chase right now regardless of how bullish the reclaim looks - no base has formed yet. The old resistance cluster (0.0286-0.0300) is now a candidate support zone if it gets retested and holds, but that hasn't happened yet either.
USUSDT.P.btc_eth_filter: Aligned bullish, but nowhere close to the driver. BTC +3.37% ($64,681), ETH +5.24% ($1,878.65) per Bybit today - both green, but US's own +22% move is roughly 4-7x the majors' move. This is coin-specific, not beta.
USUSDT.P.catalyst: Unverified. No US-specific news checked; a move this size without a confirmed catalyst is itself a flag - treat as News Risk-adjacent until checked, not Clear.

entry.basis: No valid entry yet. Neither has happened yet - this is a fresh, unmapped structure as of this read.

USUSDT.P.invalidation: N/A - no live entry to invalidate. Watch for: a base forming (multi-candle consolidation, not one candle), or a retest of 0.0286-0.0300 that holds as support (bullish continuation) vs. fails and closes back below (bull trap / fade back into range).
USUSDT.P.nearest_liquidity: The former resistance cluster (0.0286-0.0300, the same one this ledger tracked for two days as a short target) is the first real structure below current price and the most likely retest zone if this pulls back. Below that, 0.0270 (yesterday's rejection zone) and the 0.0217 base. Above current price is untested air - no prior structure, since this is a fresh high.
USUSDT.P.reclaim_status: Reclaimed decisively: price closed through the entire 0.027-0.0309 structure and is holding well above it (~0.0332 vs. 0.0309 SL line). This is a genuine reclaim, not a wick - the old resistance cluster's role has flipped and it is now a candidate support zone, not proven yet since it hasn't been retested from above.

This is real coin-specific strength, not correlation, which raises the stakes on the unverified-catalyst flag above - big relative moves like this are usually catalyst-driven even when the catalyst hasn't surfaced on TradingView yet.
USUSDT.P.final_action: WAIT / NO CHASE - Parabolic Spike Rule applies. Do not chase the move. Watch for a base or a held retest of 0.0286-0.0300 before considering any long entry.
USUSDT.P.notes: 2026-07-14 fresh reanalysis (supersedes the dead 07-07 origin-base long thesis - TP3 already tagged Jul 9 per the on-chart card, journaled below). TradingView hit its known interval-switch render bug repeatedly this session; SL widened after review from 0.0296 to 0.0309 to clear the real 07-09 19:00-07-10 07:00 supply cluster (true top 0.0300, not the understated 0.0285-0.0288). Correct short convention (entry low, 0.027) put TP1 at 1.36R, below the 2R floor - setup was WAIT/SKIP even on a clean rejection. 2026-07-14 correction: the 11:00 candle was a valid single-candle rejection (close 0.026272, fully below the 0.027 floor), not merely a touch - reasoning fixed, call unchanged (still WAIT/SKIP, R:R floor was the real blocker). 2026-07-15 10:33 UTC full regime-change reanalysis: the short thesis is dead. Price broke through the entire zone structure and the SL level in one parabolic move (~0.026 to ~0.033, +22% today), confirmed both on the live Pine card (SL TAGGED, Jul 15 07:30) and the raw candles (one violent breakout candle, no pause). Direction flipped to long-biased per the reclaim, but Parabolic Spike Rule blocks any entry until a base forms or the old resistance cluster (0.0286-0.0300) gets retested from above and holds. Cleared entry/SL/TP fields rather than guess at a level with zero structure to back it - this is intentionally a 'no clean setup yet' record, not a placeholder trade.

journal.result: WIN - closed manually at 0.6051, entry 0.665, SL 0.695 (never touched). Realized ~2.0R, which cleared the corrected TP1 (0.6076) despite the exit being a manual close rather than a TP fill - 0.6051 is a better price than 0.6076 for a short. Held roughly three days (entered 07-13, closed 07-16) through two separate zone tests (a wick-only reclaim sweep above 0.68 that got rejected, and a later sweep-and-reclaim of the TP1 zone itself) without the thesis ever actually invalidating (SL untouched throughout).
journal.note: This trade carries two separate, opposite lessons that should not be merged into one takeaway. (1) Entry timing was still premature at the start - the original limit filled ~15-30 min before the actual confirmation candle closed (documented in SCORECARD.json's JTO record), which was flagged as a real process issue at the time, independent of outcome. (2) TP levels were miscalibrated THREE times over the trade's life as the TP Zone Validity Rule and its near-edge refinement were discovered and sharpened (0.59 -> 0.5968 -> 0.6076 for TP1 alone) - every correction moved the stated R:R down, never up, which became the general pattern named explicitly in PUMPFUN's record. Despite both of these process gaps, the underlying directional thesis (short from the 0.665-0.68 reclaim-failure zone) was correct and the trade won on a real, if early, discretionary exit. Do not let a winning outcome retroactively excuse either the early entry or the TP miscalibration - grade process and outcome separately, same principle established in the scorecard for this exact trade.

Verify by reading the rendered card, not by absence of the compile-error icon alone.
